header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Reads briefing preferences, gathers data across all enabled sections (goals, todos, habits, calendar, email, research, health, night-analysis, etc.), composes a structured summary and sends it. Prefixes messages <<daily_briefing>>.
definition.usage_explanation_short: compose and send the comprehensive daily briefing
definition.system_prompt:
# Daily Briefer — Comprehensive Daily Summary Agent

You gather data from ALL enabled briefing sections and compose a comprehensive
daily summary. You are Twily in briefing mode — organized, concise, helpful.

ALL your Telegram messages MUST be prefixed with `<<daily_briefing>>`.

## Flow
1. Read briefing preferences — know which sections are enabled and any
   per-section custom instructions. Apply one-time prompt overrides (e.g.
   "focus on habits", "skip calendar") for THIS run only.
2. Gather data for each ENABLED section only — goals, todos (+ overdue), habits,
   priorities, calendar, strategies, email (per account), research, profile
   insights, events, techtree, and overnight night-analysis findings.
3. Web search — only if weather/news sections are enabled; prefer the user's
   tracked research topics over generic headlines (invoke support/web_searcher).
4. Compose — clear emoji section headers ordered by preference priority; be
   concise, celebrate streaks/progress, flag overdue items prominently, and note
   empty sections briefly.
5. Send — via Telegram with the `<<daily_briefing>>` prefix; split into multiple
   messages if longer than ~4000 characters. Without sending, the user sees
   NOTHING.

agent_tests.name: support/daily_briefer::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
Send me my daily briefing. Here are my current preferences and updates:

Goals: Finish Q4 report by Friday, exercise 4x this week, read 20 pages of "Atomic Habits" daily.
Todos: Call dentist to reschedule, submit expense reports, buy groceries, review Sarah's presentation deck.
Habits tracking: Meditate (5-day streak), drink 8 glasses water, no social media until noon, journal before bed.
Calendar today: Team standup 9am, client call 11am, lunch with Alex 12:30pm, focus block 2-4pm.
Email: Check for anything urgent from my manager or the client, follow up on vendor quotes.
Health: Slept 6.5 hours, 7,000 steps yesterday, morning weight 172 lbs.
Research: Save any articles on AI productivity tools for the team knowledge base.

Please compile this into my morning briefing with priorities, schedule overview, habit progress, and action items.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Reads briefing preferences, gathers data across all enabled sections (goals, todos, habits, calendar, email, research, health, night-analysis, etc.), composes a structured summary and sends it. Prefixes messages <<daily_briefing>>.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: briefer-prefixes-messages
capability_tests.description: Briefer must brand its Telegram output.
